rationale.R1.1: The artifact contains explicit thesis (orchestration substrate as binding constraint), a gap statement ('to our knowledge, first'), and a multi-part contribution claim (role registry + scheduler + workspace); any general 'check' on a research abstract centrally involves verifying these are coherent and well-grounded.
rationale.R1.2: The paper constructs a causal argument—checkpointing drives the performance gap—and links it through the rollback mechanism to the 5-of-7 sub-claim; checking whether the inferential chain from architecture design to benchmark outcome holds is load-bearing for a research artifact review.
rationale.R1.3: Concrete quantitative claims are present (24/32 vs 17/32; gap attributed to checkpointing in 5 of 7 additional successes); verifying that stated results actually support the attribution claim is a natural and central obligation of 'check this' on an empirical systems paper.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I perform a full scientific review (scrutinizing logic, evidence-claim alignment, and the novelty 'first' assertion), or is the primary goal a writing and prose quality check?
